what_it_does: Defines the central frozen domain data object window.MS_DATA for the Counterpart Studio prototype, including projects, lifecycle phases, sequences, states, mockups, provider/model metadata, default routing, navigation zones and pages, templates, extracted design maps, package readiness, Vi suggestions, and operations mock data, plus a few accessor helpers.
observations: A single IIFE assigns window.MS_DATA and exposes helper functions.
Entity shapes are documented to mirror a Supabase schema in mockup-studio-v0.1.md.
Defines 10 lifecycle phases from concept to live, each with icon and subtitle.
Active project p_vi is in review phase with five sequences and a seven-state Cost Impact spine.
Mockups include approved canonical frames, style anchors, six review candidates with QA rule results, and two rejected memory examples.
Providers array covers OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, and Ollama with model costs, latency, strengths, limits, and release dates.
defaultRouting maps job types like next_frame_with_refs and fast_variations to preferred models.
Navigation is modeled twice: zones/pages for the main spine drawer and workspaceGroups/workspaces for project-level surfaces.
Templates catalog includes renderers, sequences, packages, and QA rulesets with usage counts.
extractedMaps and packageState formalize handoff artifacts for Claude Design.
viSuggestions is keyed by workspace/context to show context-aware assistant prompts.
opsData is truncated but shows live KPIs and recent events.
Some computed strings reference external globals: window.MS_STATUS, window.MS_DECISIONS, window.MS_KINDS, window.MS_STREAMS.
standouts: Candidate mockups carry structured per-rule QA results (pass/warn/fail) and a vi_pick flag.
Rejected mockups are paired with a reusable anti-pattern memory list.
Provider metadata includes speculative future model names, pricing, and latency.
Extracted maps decompose a mockup into scene brief, style map, layer map, semantic map, motion map, interaction map, asset manifest, and QA rules.
Vi suggestions are tailored to each workspace surface.
conventions: IIFE used to expose only window.MS_DATA.
Uppercase constants for immutable lookup arrays such as PHASES.
Mixed function styles: const arrow functions and function declarations.
Heavy narrative block comments explaining domain intent.
Status labels and tones resolved through window.MS_STATUS rather than hardcoded.
ID prefixes encode entity kind: p_, s_, st_, m_, rp_, b_, t_.
Asset paths are relative to assets/mockups/.
concerns: The file reads globals (window.MS_STATUS, MS_DECISIONS, MS_KINDS, MS_STREAMS) that are not defined here; if absent, some computed strings will be undefined or throw.
zonePages status.text expressions are evaluated once at IIFE time, so later registry updates will not refresh them.
Two overlapping navigation taxonomies (zones/zonePages and workspaceGroups/workspaces) may drift.
packageState.sequence_complete and readme_drafted are false while one project is in package phase, which may be intentional pending work or inconsistency.
notes: This is the prototype's single source of structured mock state: every UI surface reads from it, and it intentionally mixes canonical data, candidate review data, model metadata, navigation config, and handoff artifacts in one place.
questions: Are workspaceGroups/workspaces meant to replace zones/zonePages or to coexist?
Is window.MS_STATUS guaranteed to load before this IIFE executes?
Should zonePages status.text expressions recompute when MS_DECISIONS/MS_KINDS/MS_STREAMS change?
Are provider model costs, latencies, and release dates real or speculative?
Is opsData complete elsewhere or intentionally truncated?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to hold all mock state and mirror a Supabase schema; actual content does that but also embeds navigation config, model bench metadata, handoff artifacts, and Vi suggestions, making it a broader central data catalog than a pure domain model.
apparent_themes: Lifecycle-driven creative production from concept to live deployment.
Generative AI as a design partner with model routing and QA scoring.
Canonical truth, candidate variation, and anti-pattern memory.
Visual-to-code handoff via structured maps and Claude Design packages.
Provider-agnostic model bench with per-job routing.
intention_signals: The file tries to model Counterpart Studio as an end-to-end idea-to-live-product workspace where a team defines sequences, generates and reviews visual candidates against style anchors and QA rules, extracts design maps, and bundles everything for downstream prototyping, with Vi acting as a context-aware assistant.
novelty: Treats rejected mockups and anti-patterns as reusable design memory, embeds a model bench with per-job routing, and structures handoff artifacts (scene/style/layer/motion/interaction maps) as first-class data rather than ad-hoc documents.
contribution: Provides a concrete, queryable mock dataset that demonstrates the intended entity shapes, lifecycle phases, review QA structure, provider/model metadata, navigation taxonomy, and handoff artifact schema for the Counterpart Studio prototype.
summary_for_embedding: Central frozen mock data module for Counterpart Studio defining projects, phases, sequences, states, mockups with QA scores, provider model metadata, zone navigation, templates, extracted design maps, package readiness, and context-aware Vi suggestions.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the prototype's single source of domain state and schema example, supporting UI development for project lifecycle, generative mockup review, model routing, and Claude Design handoff.

symbols.does: Looks up a lifecycle phase by id, falling back to the first phase.
symbols.description: A small accessor that returns the matching PHASES entry or the concept phase as a safe default, used by UI surfaces that need phase metadata.

symbols.does: Returns the zero-based index of a phase id, clamped to zero.
symbols.description: Used to compute project progress or position along the lifecycle journey; returns 0 for unknown ids so the UI never lands on a negative step.

symbols.does: Returns metadata for a zone id, including the account pseudo-zone fallback.
symbols.description: Resolves a zone id to its label, icon, and blurb; if the id is account it returns a synthetic entry, otherwise it falls back to the first zone.

symbols.does: Finds which zone owns a given page id.
symbols.description: Scans zonePages and returns the zone id whose page list contains the requested page, defaulting to project when no match is found.

symbols.does: Returns the first page id in a zone's page list.
symbols.description: A convenience used when selecting a zone to know which page to show by default; returns undefined for unknown zones.

symbols.does: Looks up metadata for a specific page id across all zones.
symbols.description: Iterates zonePages to find the page object matching the id, returning null if the page is not registered in any zone.

symbols.does: Derives a flat file list from the mockups array.
symbols.description: Filters mockups that have a src, then labels each file's kind by consulting window.MS_STATUS for style_anchor, candidate, and rejected statuses, otherwise defaulting to Mockup.

symbols.does: Maps a mockup status to a file kind label via MS_STATUS.
symbols.description: An inner helper used by buildFiles; it treats style_anchor, candidate, and rejected as special statuses resolved through the external window.MS_STATUS registry, and falls back to Mockup for everything else.
